user_query:
Please read the list of device identifiers in input/serials.csv (column name: serial). For each value, classify it and produce structured results as follows:

1) Classification rules:
- If it is a 12-character old-format Apple serial with a valid year/week code, classify format as "old_12char" and decode manufacturing location, manufacture date (year, half, and week_number), and try to identify the device using the model code (last 3-4 characters) when possible.
- If it is a 10-character post-2021 randomized serial, classify format as "new_randomized" and include a note that it cannot be decoded locally.
- If it is a 15-digit IMEI, classify format as "imei" and include an error message that IMEI is not an Apple serial number.

2) Write a line-delimited JSON file at output/decoded.jsonl with one JSON object per input value. Each object must include these fields (exact keys):
- serial (string)
- format (one of: "old_12char", "new_randomized", "imei")
- For format = "old_12char":
  - manufacturing_location: { code: string, name: string }
  - manufacture_date: { year: number, half: "H1"|"H2", week_number: number }
  - device_info: if known, { model_identifier: string, device_name: string } else { status: string }
  - last_four: string (last 4 characters of the serial)
- For format = "new_randomized": include a field note: string explaining it cannot be decoded locally
- For format = "imei": include a field error: string explaining it's not a serial number

3) Write a JSON summary at output/metrics.json with the following structure:
{
  "total": <number>,
  "old_12char": [<serials>],
  "new_randomized": [<serials>],
  "imei": [<serials>]
}
The arrays must list the serials in each category, and total must equal the total count of all input values.

4) Write a human-readable Markdown report at output/summary.md that:
- Summarizes how many items fell into each category
- Lists each serial with a one-line result (device name if known for old-format; note for randomized; error for IMEI)
- Includes a section titled "Next steps" that advises what to do for new randomized serials (e.g., use Apple’s official coverage lookup) and for any unknown old-format model codes

Notes:
- Be careful to distinguish IMEIs (15 digits) from serials.
- For old-format serials, ensure the manufacturing location code and year/week decode are consistent with the serial structure.
- Use known model code mappings when available.

Write the three files to the output/ directory exactly as specified.
